challenges.description: RISK-008 (TeamCity → Azure Pipelines) rated medium severity but has highest hours impact (24h). Severity/impact misalignment.
challenges.resolution: Recommend upgrading RISK-008 to high severity or reducing hours to 16hr since source repos already on Azure DevOps significantly reduces per-pipeline migration effort.

challenges.description: Azure DMS classic retires March 2026. Azure Data Studio retires Feb 2026. RISK-001 mitigation references 'Azure Database Migration Service' which is being deprecated.
challenges.resolution: Use Managed Instance Link for large DB migration (250GB+). Use VS Code MSSQL extension for assessment. DMS classic alternatives: MI Link uses Always On technology for near-zero downtime.

challenges.description: many_sites multiplier (1.5x) based on assumed >10 sites. No explicit site count in discovery. 20 CDN distributions could be multi-language distributions for fewer sites.
challenges.resolution: 20 CDN distributions + ~30 repos strongly suggests >10 sites. Multiplier kept but flagged as assumption requiring validation of exact site count.

challenges.description: multi_environment multiplier on cicd_pipeline (1.3x on 16hr base) plus RISK-008 (24hrs for 30 pipeline configs) may double-count CI/CD effort.
challenges.resolution: Base effort and risk buffer are separate. Recommend reducing RISK-008 to 16hr since Azure DevOps source mitigates effort significantly.

challenges.description: No risks identified for networking, identity, monitoring, or backup_dr dimensions despite being in-scope components.
challenges.resolution: These dimensions have standard, low-complexity migration paths. Not every dimension warrants explicit risks.

challenges.description: Discovery says Solr 8.8 but Sitecore 10.4 supports 8.11.2. Solr 8.x is EOL since June 2024.
challenges.resolution: Verify actual Solr version. Solr 8.x EOL since June 2024. Consider upgrading to Sitecore 10.4.1 + Solr 9.8.1 during Azure migration. Solr on Azure VM confirmed as correct approach for 30+ custom indexes.

challenges.description: RISK-002 (media blobs in SQL) rated medium severity but 16hr impact is borderline for medium.
challenges.resolution: 16hr for 50GB+ media blob migration is reasonable. Medium severity appropriate — manageable with planning, not a blocker.

challenges.description: redis_ssl_default and serialization_large_item_tree gotcha patterns unevaluable due to discovery gaps.
challenges.resolution: Document as minor risks with 4hr contingency each. Redis SSL migration is near-certain (Azure Cache requires SSL).

challenges.description: Azure Cache for Redis tier recommendation needs verification for Sitecore 10.4.
challenges.resolution: Azure Cache for Redis Premium P1 (6GB) confirmed. Async session state provider default since 10.1. No changes needed.

challenges.description: No VPN assumed but hybrid AWS↔Azure connectivity needed during migration for data sync and testing.
challenges.resolution: Hybrid connectivity IS needed during migration. Add medium-severity risk for temporary AWS↔Azure VPN or public endpoint configuration during data sync period.

challenges.description: Sitecore license re-keying for Azure hosting not captured in risks or assumptions.
challenges.resolution: Sitecore licensing for IaaS (VM) deployments typically doesn't require re-keying. Low risk. Add as verification checklist item.

findings.finding: Azure DMS classic retires March 15, 2026. Azure Data Studio + SQL Migration extension also retires February 28, 2026. Microsoft recommends VS Code with MSSQL extension. For large databases (250GB+), Managed Instance Link using Always On technology is the preferred approach.
findings.recommendation: Update RISK-001 mitigation to reference MI Link instead of DMS.

findings.finding: Sitecore 10.4 officially supports Solr 8.11.2 (not 8.8). Solr 8.x is EOL since June 2024. Sitecore 10.4.1 supports Solr 9.8.1. Azure AI Search has index limits that won't support 30+ custom indexes. Solr on Azure VM confirmed as correct approach.
findings.recommendation: Verify actual Solr version. Consider 10.4.1 upgrade with Solr 9.8.1 during migration.

findings.finding: Azure Cache for Redis Premium P1 (6GB) confirmed as recommended for Sitecore session state. Async session state provider default since 10.1. VNet integration available with Premium.

summary: Analysis challenge review passed at 83%. 11 challenges generated (2 critical, 4 high, 5 medium). 9 resolved, 2 accepted. Key findings: (1) Azure DMS classic retiring March 2026 — use MI Link for large DB migration; (2) Solr 8.8 in discovery should be verified — Sitecore 10.4 supports 8.11.2, and 8.x is EOL; consider 10.4.1 + Solr 9.8.1; (3) Azure Cache for Redis Premium confirmed correct; (4) Hybrid AWS↔Azure connectivity needed during migration; (5) RISK-008 CI/CD severity should be upgraded to high or hours reduced.
